addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

3200	Update the sliding hash value with the given byte. All calls to this function
3201	must be made on consecutive input characters. Since the hash value exists out
3202	of multiple input bytes, a few warmups with this function are needed initially.
3203	*/
3204	static void UpdateHashValue(ZopfliHash *h, unsigned char c)
3205	/* The hash object itself must be a valid, writable object. */
3206	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3207	/* The running hash value is always the result of a previous masking with
3208	   HASH_MASK (or zero-initialized), hence in [0, HASH_MASK]. This invariant
3209	   guarantees that `h->val << HASH_SHIFT` (at most HASH_MASK << 5 = 1048544)
3210	   does not overflow the signed `int`. */
3211	__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3212	/* Only the current hash value is updated. */
3213	__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
3214	/* The masking re-establishes the [0, HASH_MASK] invariant for the next call. */
3215	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3216	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == ((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ (c)) & HASH_MASK))
3217	{
3218	    h->val = (((h->val) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ (c)) & HASH_MASK;
3219	}
3220	
3221	/*
3222	Prepopulates hash:
3223	Fills in the initial values in the hash, before ZopfliUpdateHash can be used
3224	correctly.
3225	*/
3226	void ZopfliWarmupHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
3227	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3228	{
3229	    UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 0]);
3230	    if (pos + 1 < end)
3231	        UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 1]);
3232	}
3233	
3234	/*
3235	Appends the length and distance to the LZ77 arrays of the ZopfliLZ77Store.
3236	context must be a ZopfliLZ77Store*.
3237	*/
3238	void ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(unsigned short length, unsigned short dist,
3239	                           size_t pos, ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
3240	/* The store itself is a valid, readable object. */
3241	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
3242	/* `length` must be a valid DEFLATE length so ZopfliGetLengthSymbol's [0, 258]
3243	   precondition holds and the (length < 259) assertion succeeds; it is also used
3244	   directly as a histogram index, which stays below ZOPFLI_NUM_LL. */
3245	__CPROVER_requires(length <= 258)
3246	/* `dist == 0` marks a literal; a real distance must be a valid DEFLATE distance
3247	   so ZopfliGetDistSymbol's [1, 32768] precondition holds. */
3248	__CPROVER_requires(dist <= 32768)
3249	/* Keep `size` non-zero and not a power of two: every ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA of a
3250	   single element (litlens, dists, pos, ll_symbol, d_symbol) then stays on its
3251	   no-reallocation path, writing in place at index `size`. */
3252	__CPROVER_requires(store->size != 0 &&
3253	                   ((store->size & (store->size - 1)) != 0))
3254	/* Keep `size` off the chunk boundaries so the two cumulative-histogram block
3255	   appends (the `size % ZOPFLI_NUM_LL == 0` / `% ZOPFLI_NUM_D == 0` branches,
3256	   which would reallocate ll_counts / d_counts) are skipped: those arrays are
3257	   only read and incremented in place on this path. */
3258	__CPROVER_requires(store->size % ZOPFLI_NUM_LL != 0)
3259	__CPROVER_requires(store->size % ZOPFLI_NUM_D != 0)
3260	/* Bound `size` so the allocation-size arithmetic below cannot overflow. */
3261	__CPROVER_requires(store->size <=
3262	                   ((~(size_t)0) / (2 * sizeof(size_t))) - ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)
3263	/* Each single-append array holds the existing `size` elements plus room for the
3264	   one element appended at index `size`. */
3265	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->litlens,
3266	                   (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->litlens)))
3267	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->dists,
3268	                   (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->dists)))
3269	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->pos,
3270	                   (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->pos)))
3271	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->ll_symbol,
3272	                   (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->ll_symbol)))
3273	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->d_symbol,
3274	                   (store->size + 1) * sizeof(*store->d_symbol)))
3275	/* The cumulative histograms are indexed at llstart/dstart + symbol, where the
3276	   start is the chunk base ZOPFLI_NUM_* * (size / ZOPFLI_NUM_*) and the symbol is
3277	   below ZOPFLI_NUM_*; they must hold the whole current chunk. */
3278	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->ll_counts,
3279	                   (ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * (store->size / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)
3280	                       * sizeof(*store->ll_counts)))
3281	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->d_counts,
3282	                   (ZOPFLI_NUM_D * (store->size / ZOPFLI_NUM_D) + ZOPFLI_NUM_D)
3283	                       * sizeof(*store->d_counts)))
3284	__CPROVER_assigns(store->size,
3285	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->litlens),
3286	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->dists),
3287	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->pos),
3288	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->ll_symbol),
3289	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->d_symbol),
3290	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->ll_counts),
3291	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->d_counts))
3292	/* Exactly one LZ77 symbol is appended. */
3293	__CPROVER_ensures(store->size == __CPROVER_old(store->size) + 1)
3294	{
3295	    size_t i;
3296	    /* Needed for using ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA multiple times. */
3297	    size_t origsize = store->size;
3298	    size_t llstart = ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * (origsize / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL);
3299	    size_t dstart = ZOPFLI_NUM_D * (origsize / ZOPFLI_NUM_D);
3300	
3301	    /* Everytime the index wraps around, a new cumulative histogram is made: we're
3302	    keeping one histogram value per LZ77 symbol rather than a full histogram for
3303	    each to save memory. */
3304	    if (origsize % ZOPFLI_NUM_LL == 0)
3305	    {
3306	        size_t llsize = origsize;
3307	        for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL; i++)
3308	        {
3309	            ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(
3310	                origsize == 0 ? 0 : store->ll_counts[origsize - ZOPFLI_NUM_LL + i],
3311	                &store->ll_counts, &llsize);
3312	        }
3313	    }
3314	    if (origsize % ZOPFLI_NUM_D == 0)
3315	    {
3316	        size_t dsize = origsize;
3317	        for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_NUM_D; i++)
3318	        {
3319	            ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(
3320	                origsize == 0 ? 0 : store->d_counts[origsize - ZOPFLI_NUM_D + i],
3321	                &store->d_counts, &dsize);
3322	        }
3323	    }
3324	
3325	    ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(length, &store->litlens, &store->size);
3326	    store->size = origsize;
3327	    ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(dist, &store->dists, &store->size);
3328	    store->size = origsize;
3329	    ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(pos, &store->pos, &store->size);
3330	    assert(length < 259);
3331	
3332	    if (dist == 0)
3333	    {
3334	        store->size = origsize;
3335	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(length, &store->ll_symbol, &store->size);
3336	        store->size = origsize;
3337	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(0, &store->d_symbol, &store->size);
3338	        store->ll_counts[llstart + length]++;
3339	    }
3340	    else
3341	    {
3342	        store->size = origsize;
3343	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(ZopfliGetLengthSymbol(length),
3344	                           &store->ll_symbol, &store->size);
3345	        store->size = origsize;
3346	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(ZopfliGetDistSymbol(dist),
3347	                           &store->d_symbol, &store->size);
3348	        store->ll_counts[llstart + ZopfliGetLengthSymbol(length)]++;
3349	        store->d_counts[dstart + ZopfliGetDistSymbol(dist)]++;
3350	    }
3351	}
3352	
3353	void ZopfliUpdateHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
3354	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3355	/* The hash object itself is a valid, writable object. */
3356	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3357	/* UpdateHashValue's precondition: the running hash value is always the result of
3358	   a previous HASH_MASK masking (or zero-initialized), hence in [0, HASH_MASK]. */
3359	__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)

3353	void ZopfliUpdateHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
3354	                      ZopfliHash *h)
3355	/* The hash object itself is a valid, writable object. */
3356	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
3357	/* UpdateHashValue's precondition: the running hash value is always the result of
3358	   a previous HASH_MASK masking (or zero-initialized), hence in [0, HASH_MASK]. */
3359	__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3360	/* head/head2 are the hash-to-index tables; they are indexed only by the hash
3361	   values, which are masked into [0, HASH_MASK], so HASH_MASK + 1 entries suffice. */
3362	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, (size_t)(HASH_MASK + 1) * sizeof(*h->head)))
3363	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, (size_t)(HASH_MASK + 1) * sizeof(*h->head2)))
3364	/* The real algorithmic precondition is that every head/head2 table entry is
3365	   either the empty marker -1 or a valid window slot in [0, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE),
3366	   which keeps the chained `hashval[head[val]]` / `hashval2[head2[val2]]` reads
3367	   (guarded by `!= -1`) in bounds. That invariant can only be written as a
3368	   `__CPROVER_forall` over each 32768-entry table; under the SAT backend such a
3369	   constant-bound quantifier is expanded into one conjunct per index, forcing the
3370	   whole table to be concretized. With both tables that makes verification time
3371	   out, and it gates no extra checked property here: the head index feeding each
3372	   chained read is the running hash *after* UpdateHashValue, an arbitrary value in
3373	   [0, HASH_MASK] that no precondition over the prior state can pin down. The
3374	   memory-layout (`is_fresh`) and functional invariants below are what this
3375	   contract verifies. */
3376	/* The per-window-slot arrays each hold ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE entries and are indexed
3377	   by hpos = pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK, i.e. in [0, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE). */
3378	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->hashval)))
3379	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->hashval2)))
3380	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->prev)))
3381	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->prev2)))
3382	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->same)))
3383	/* The input data is a readable buffer of `end` bytes. All array reads in the body
3384	   are guarded by an index strictly below `end`. */
3385	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end))
3386	/* The processed position lies within the data, as at every call site. */
3387	__CPROVER_requires(pos < end)
3388	/* Only the current hash slot's bookkeeping and the two running hash values are
3389	   updated; the touched arrays are framed as whole objects. */
3390	__CPROVER_assigns(h->val, h->val2,
3391	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval),
3392	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2),
3393	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev),
3394	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2),
3395	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->same),
3396	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head),
3397	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2))
3398	/* Both running hash values stay masked into [0, HASH_MASK], preserving the
3399	   invariant the next call (and UpdateHashValue) relies on. */
3400	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
3401	__CPROVER_ensures(h->val2 >= 0 && h->val2 <= HASH_MASK)
3402	{
3403	    unsigned short hpos = pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK;
3404	    size_t amount = 0;
3405	
3406	    UpdateHashValue(h, pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0);
3407	    h->hashval[hpos] = h->val;
3408	    if (h->head[h->val] != -1 && h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] == h->val)
3409	    {
3410	        h->prev[hpos] = h->head[h->val];
3411	    }
3412	    else
3413	        h->prev[hpos] = hpos;
3414	    h->head[h->val] = hpos;
3415	
3416	    /* Update "same". */
3417	    if (h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] > 1)
3418	    {
3419	        amount = h->same[(pos - 1) & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] - 1;
3420	    }
3421	    while (pos + amount + 1 < end &&
3422	           array[pos] == array[pos + amount + 1] && amount < (unsigned short)(-1))
3423	    __CPROVER_assigns(amount)
3424	    /* `amount` is only ever incremented while strictly below its (unsigned short)
3425	       cap, so it stays representable as the unsigned short stored into same[]. */
3426	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(amount <= (size_t)(unsigned short)(-1))
3427	    /* Each iteration strictly increases amount toward the cap, bounding the loop. */
3428	    __CPROVER_decreases((size_t)(unsigned short)(-1) - amount)
3429	    {
3430	        amount++;
3431	    }
3432	    h->same[hpos] = amount;
3433	
3434	    h->val2 = ((h->same[hpos] - ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH) & 255) ^ h->val;
3435	    h->hashval2[hpos] = h->val2;
3436	    if (h->head2[h->val2] != -1 && h->hashval2[h->head2[h->val2]] == h->val2)
3437	    {
3438	        h->prev2[hpos] = h->head2[h->val2];
3439	    }
3440	    else
3441	        h->prev2[hpos] = hpos;
3442	    h->head2[h->val2] = hpos;
3443	}
3444	
3445	void ZopfliResetHash(size_t window_size, ZopfliHash *h)
3446	/* window_size is bounded by the actual window used by all callers; the upper
3447	   bound also keeps the index i within unsigned short range, so prev[i] == i. */

content.text: Now I understand the patterns. Let me write the contract for `ZopfliWarmupHash`.

content.content: The file /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: void ZopfliWarmupHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
ZopfliHash *h)
+/* The hash object itself is a valid, writable object. */
+__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
+/* UpdateHashValue's precondition: the running hash value is the result of a
+   previous HASH_MASK masking (or zero-initialized), hence in [0, HASH_MASK]. */
+__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
+/* The input data is a readable buffer of `end` bytes. The unconditional read at
+   array[pos] is kept in bounds by pos < end; the array[pos + 1] read is guarded
+   by pos + 1 < end. */
+__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end))
+/* The warmup position lies within the data, as at every call site. */
+__CPROVER_requires(pos < end)
+/* Only the running hash value is updated (via UpdateHashValue). */
+__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
+/* The running hash value stays masked into [0, HASH_MASK], preserving the
+   invariant the next call (and UpdateHashValue) relies on. */
+__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 0]);
if (pos + 1 < end)

content.text:
Verification succeeded. `ZopfliWarmupHash` now has a contract that CBMC verifies.

The contract captures:
- **`h` is a fresh, writable hash object** and **`h->val ∈ [0, HASH_MASK]`** — both required by the callee `UpdateHashValue`.
- **`array` is a readable buffer of `end` bytes** with **`pos < end`** — keeps the unconditional `array[pos]` read in bounds; the `array[pos + 1]` read is internally guarded by `pos + 1 < end`.
- **assigns** only `h->val`, and **ensures** `h->val ∈ [0, HASH_MASK]`, preserving the masking invariant that subsequent `UpdateHashValue`/`ZopfliUpdateHash` calls depend on.

This matches the established conventions of the neighboring `UpdateHashValue` and `ZopfliUpdateHash` contracts in the same file.
